sources.notes: Statement asks for the total binary-search comparison count over all nondecreasing arrays of length n with values in [1,m].
Official editorial gives two O(n) approaches: Vandermonde plus interval minima with a monotonic stack, and inclusion-exclusion over binary-search recursion intervals.
Luogu article used to cross-check the contribution decomposition, stars-and-bars interpretation, and Vandermonde simplification.

reference_candidates.notes: Candidate Luogu solution index; the article listed in sources was the actually consulted Luogu source.

annotation.summary: Sum, over all nondecreasing arrays a of length n with values in [1,m], the total number of binary-search comparisons needed to find every present value.
annotation.constraints: Total n and total m across tests are each <= 1e6, so linear preprocessing and linear per-test processing are intended.
annotation.core_idea: The binary-search recursion tree fixes a depth for each array index. For a maximal equal-value block [l,r], the search cost for that value is the minimum depth inside [l,r]. For an interior value x, stars-and-bars counts choices on the left and right as C(l+x-3,x-2) * C(m-x+n-r-1,m-x-1); Vandermonde collapses the sum over x so the coefficient depends only on block length. The remaining task is summing minimum depths over all intervals of each length, computed by a monotonic stack and second-order difference updates. Edge values 1 and m are handled separately by prefix/suffix minimum depths.
annotation.complexity: O(n) per test case after O(max(n,m)) factorial/inverse precomputation.
tricks: block contribution by minimum binary-search depth

solution_variants.name: vandermonde-min-intervals
solution_variants.summary: Build the binary-search depth array, reduce equal-value block contribution to a length-dependent binomial coefficient using stars-and-bars and Vandermonde, then compute all interval-minimum sums by monotonic stack plus second-order differences.
solution_variants.complexity: O(n) per test case after factorial precomputation

solution_variants.name: recursion-interval-inclusion-exclusion
solution_variants.summary: Traverse binary-search recursion intervals and count arrays where the searched value terminates at the midpoint by stars-and-bars with inclusion-exclusion on boundary equalities.
solution_variants.complexity: O(n) per test case after factorial precomputation

tags.evidence: The primary solution uses stars-and-bars and Vandermonde's identity to collapse a value sum into a coefficient depending only on interval length.
After the combinatorial reduction, the central task is summing minimum binary-search depths over all intervals, solved by monotonic-stack dominance ranges.
Each depth contributes a triangular count pattern over interval lengths, accumulated with second-order difference updates.
The alternative official solution subtracts arrays where the left or right strict boundary fails, then adds back their intersection.
Both solutions rely on the fixed binary-search recursion tree over intervals.
